metadata.description: Organization-level information vaults — categories of information that ANY persona acting on behalf of that organization should not reveal in an email correspondence. Used at runtime by the predictive classifier: a generic persona simulating a response 'as part of' a given organization is bound by that organization's vault. Per-persona vaults in phishing_response_personas.json layer on role-specific additions; this file captures the shared baseline.

schema.id: Matches the organization id in phishing_response_personas.json.
schema.name: Human-readable organization name.
schema.industry: Short description of the org's industry.
schema.regulatory_environment: Relevant compliance regimes (HIPAA, PCI-DSS, FERPA, SOX, GLBA, etc.) that constrain disclosure.
schema.information_vault: List of information categories that no member of the organization should reveal over email to an unverified counterparty. Each entry has a `category` slug and a plain-language `description`.
schema.applies_to: Whether this vault applies to all personas in the org ('all') or a named subset. Default is 'all'.

metadata.notes_for_unaffiliated: Personas whose `organization_id` is null are unaffiliated (consumer) personas. They have no organization vault — their disclosure rules are entirely defined by their per-persona information_vault.

organizations.name: Meridian Manufacturing Co.
organizations.regulatory_environment: SOX (financial controls), export controls on certain components, supplier-confidentiality NDAs

information_vault.description: Meridian's outgoing bank account and wire-transfer credentials; SWIFT/IBAN of operating accounts.
Bank routing/account numbers on file for any vendor; instructions for changing them.
Approval thresholds, dual-control rules, names of approvers, and which steps can be skipped under pressure.
ERP, email, and VPN passwords; MFA codes; one-time tokens; SSO recovery questions.
SSNs, home addresses, dates of birth, direct-deposit accounts, salary figures, ID document copies.
Real-time travel plans, vacation/out-of-office windows, or unavailability of executives that would enable impersonation timing.
Vendor counts, consolidation status, negotiated savings, supplier scorecards, ERP/platform details, customer concentration, and internal forecasts.
Bulk org charts or directories with names, titles, reporting lines, direct lines, and personal contact for many employees.
Outstanding invoices/POs and their status to unverified counterparties; payment dates and amounts.
Customer lists, contract terms, pricing, and any data covered by an NDA.
Halcyon's operating-account routing/account numbers and wire-initiation credentials.
Banking details on file for any carrier or vendor and the process for changing them.
AP approval thresholds, dual-control wire procedures, callback policy, and any deviations from policy.
ERP, TMS, WMS, email, and VPN credentials; MFA codes.
Employee SSNs, addresses, DOBs, direct-deposit accounts, ID copies.
CEO/CFO travel plans and out-of-office windows that would enable timing-based impersonation.
Customer shipment volumes, routes, schedules, declared values, and any contract pricing.
Pre-publication revenue, earnings, EBITDA, customer concentration, segment results.
Outstanding payables and receivables, including amounts and aging.
Bulk directories, org charts, reporting lines, and direct phone numbers.

organizations.name: Pulsar Technologies
organizations.regulatory_environment: SOC 2, customer DPAs, occasional GDPR/CCPA obligations on customer data

information_vault.description: Cloud provider keys, database credentials, Okta/IdP admin access, deploy keys, signing keys, MFA seeds.
Any data stored on behalf of a customer, including PII, content, and usage telemetry.
Repository contents, internal architecture diagrams, secrets in repos or .env files.
Cloud accounts, network topology, security tooling and versions, on-call rotation, incident-response playbooks.
SSNs, home addresses, DOBs, direct-deposit accounts, ID copies, salary figures.
Pre-quarterly financials, ARR/MRR, churn, customer-concentration data, board materials, M&A discussions.
Specifics of IT-migration steps, password-reset workflows, or any process that could be mimicked in a follow-up pretext.
Pulsar's operating bank credentials and wire-initiation authority.
Banking on file for any vendor and the process for changes.
Bulk org charts, reporting lines, employee phone numbers.

organizations.name: Stonebridge Financial Services
organizations.regulatory_environment: GLBA, SOX, BSA/AML, PCI-DSS, FFIEC examinations, customer privacy notices

information_vault.description: Any nonpublic personal information about a customer: account numbers, balances, transaction history, SSN, DOB, addresses, credentials, or anything covered by GLBA.
The bank's own operating credentials, wire authority, and signature controls.
Dual-control rules, BSA/AML thresholds, suspicious-activity workflows, exceptions, and whether any can be bypassed.
Core banking, online banking admin, AD/SSO, MFA seeds, fraud-tool credentials.
Open exam findings, regulator correspondence, internal compliance memos.
Pre-publication earnings, allowance estimates, deposit composition, loan-loss reserves.
Employee SSNs, addresses, DOBs, direct-deposit accounts, salary figures, ID copies.
Pending litigation, internal investigations, legal holds, attorney-client communications.
Active fraud investigations, internal indicators of compromise, BEC patterns recently observed.
Bulk lists of customers or employees with contact details or reporting structure.

organizations.name: Cedarview Regional Health
organizations.regulatory_environment: HIPAA (PHI), HITECH, state breach-notification laws, PCI-DSS for payments, EMTALA for clinical operations

information_vault.description: Any protected health information — diagnoses, treatments, test results, visit records, MRN, insurance details, claims data, or anything that identifies a patient combined with health information.
Patient account numbers, payment-card data, balances, payment-plan terms, or anything that would enable charging or modifying a patient account.
EFT/ERA banking details, clearinghouse routing setup, insurer-payment configuration, and the process for changing them.
EHR/EMR credentials, billing-system access, AD/SSO, MFA codes, VPN, remote-support tools.
Staff SSNs, addresses, DOBs, direct-deposit, salary, ID copies, license numbers.
Clinician license numbers, DEA numbers, credentialing files, malpractice details.
Hospital operating-account credentials and wire-initiation authority.
Banking on file for vendors/insurers, contract terms, and the process for changing them.
Internal workflows, on-call schedules, security/IT processes that an attacker could mimic in follow-up pretexts.
Bulk patient/employee directories, scheduling records, or contact lists.

organizations.name: Northwind State University
organizations.regulatory_environment: FERPA (student records), GLBA (financial aid), HIPAA (campus health), Title IV (federal aid), state public-records laws

information_vault.description: Anything FERPA-protected — grades, transcripts, disciplinary records, enrollment, financial aid records, course-level performance, identifiable directory data beyond what's already public.
SSNs, FSA IDs, DOBs, home addresses, banking/direct deposit, ID copies, financial-aid eligibility detail.
SIS, LMS, payroll, financial-aid, AD/SSO, MFA, VPN, research-cluster credentials.
Unpublished research data, grant budgets, restricted-data security plans, IRB-protected data.
University operating credentials, wire-initiation authority, bursar disbursement controls.
Banking on file for any vendor and the process for changing it.
Donor giving history, advancement notes, alumni contact information.
Campus public-safety incident reports, student-health records, counseling records.
IT-migration steps, password-reset workflows, financial-aid disbursement workflow specifics.
Bulk student or employee directories with contact details and reporting structure.
